# This replicates the core logic of SegmentEditorThresholdEffect.onApply()
# without requiring a visible Segment Editor widget.

# --- Resolve scene nodes by fuzzy name ---------------------------------------
def _find_node_by_keyword(keyword, node_type):
    """Return the first scene node whose name contains `keyword`."""
    coll = slicer.mrmlScene.GetNodesByClass(node_type)
    coll.InitTraversal()
    while True:
        node = coll.GetNextItemAsObject()
        if not node:
            break
        if keyword.lower() in node.GetName().lower():
            return node
    raise RuntimeError(
        "MISSING_EVIDENCE: No {type} with name containing '{kw}' found in scene"
        .format(type=node_type, kw=keyword)
    )

segmentationNode = _find_node_by_keyword(
    "{segmentation_name_keyword: Bone}", "vtkMRMLSegmentationNode"
)
sourceVolumeNode = _find_node_by_keyword(
    "{source_volume_name_keyword: CT}", "vtkMRMLScalarVolumeNode"
)

# --- Ensure the target segment exists ----------------------------------------
segmentId = "{segment_id: Bone}"
segmentation = segmentationNode.GetSegmentation()
if not segmentation.GetSegment(segmentId):
    segment = slicer.vtkSegment()
    segment.SetName(segmentId)
    # Default bone color: white / light tan
    segment.SetColor(0.95, 0.92, 0.80)
    segmentation.AddSegment(segment, segmentId)
